Tests if the absolute value of negative infinity is positive infinity in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that verifies the behavior of the `Math.abs` function when applied to `Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY`. The test checks that the absolute value of negative infinity is equal to `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`. This is a narrow functionality test, focusing on a specific mathematical operation and its expected result in JavaScript. The code uses the `assert` function to ensure that the condition holds true, which is a common practice in testing to validate assumptions in code.
